title: Snow Particles
description: A simple interactive snowfall you can play around with.
longDescription: This is a relaxing snowfall scene where snowflakes drift down the screen at different speeds and sizes. You can interact with it by clicking or dragging to push the snow around and create little bursts. It is designed to feel smooth and responsive on any screen size.
controls: Click anywhere to create a burst in the snow
Click and drag to push the snow around
Resize the window and it will adjust automatically
notes: Different snowflakes fall at different speeds to make it feel more natural
Designed to stay smooth even with lots of particles on screen
Must have desktop display scaling set to 100% otherwise game will break

title: Rose Curves
description: Explore and play with different mathematical patterns.
longDescription: This project lets you explore rose curves, which are patterns created using maths. You can switch between different shapes, adjust how they are drawn, and watch them animate smoothly. It is part visual tool and part experiment.
controls: Use the arrows or buttons to switch between curves
Press H to hide the interface
Press S to open settings
Use the sliders to change how the curve behaves
notes: Each curve is drawn smoothly to keep the animation clean
You can tweak the settings to create your own variations
Must have desktop display scaling set to 100% otherwise game will break

title: Polygon Network
description: A rotating network of points with colourful connecting lines.
longDescription: This project draws a rotating set of points around a circle and connects every point to every other point, creating changing geometric patterns. You can adjust the number of points, change the rotation speed, toggle trails, and fill the polygon for different visual effects.
controls: Use the sliders to change speed and number of points
Click to add one point
Arrow keys adjust the point count
Space pauses and resumes the animation
R resets the rotation of the animation
T toggles trail mode
F toggles polygon fill
S saves an image
H hides or shows the help and sliders
notes: Different point counts create very different geometric patterns
Trail mode lets the motion build up into more complex visuals
Must have desktop display scaling set to 100% otherwise game will break

title: Lissajous Curves
description: Lissajous curves drawn one at a time with changing ratios.
longDescription: Each curve comes from pairing two motion ratios and drawing the result progressively. After finishing, it pauses briefly, fades out, and moves to the next ratio. The curve stays centred and fills most of the screen, with a faint guide to help track the motion.
controls: Use the settings menu to change the grid size (ratio set)
Adjust speed, pause time, fade, tip length, and guide opacity
Click Pause on the canvas to stop or resume
notes: Each curve is labelled by its ratio
Must have desktop display scaling set to 100% otherwise game will break

title: RGB Colour Match
description: Try to match the target colour before the timer runs out.
longDescription: Use the red, green, and blue sliders to match the target colour as closely as you can. When the round ends, your score is based on how close your colour was. You can change the difficulty, submit early, and try to beat your high score.
controls: Use the RGB sliders to match the target colour
Click Done to submit before time runs out
Click Reset to start a new round
Change the difficulty to adjust the timer
Press H to show or hide the help text
notes: Score is based on how close your RGB values are to the target
High score is tracked during play
The target RGB values are only shown after the round ends

title: Zenith
description: A survival shooter where you dodge enemies, level up, and build your run through upgrades.
longDescription: Zenith is a top-down survival game where you move, survive, and slowly turn your build into something stronger. You auto-fire at enemies while trying to stay alive long enough to level up and choose new upgrades. As more enemy types appear and the pace increases, runs become more chaotic and build choices start to matter more.
controls: Move with WASD or arrow keys
Auto-fire targets the nearest enemy
Use abilities when available
Pick upgrades on level up
notes: Focused on movement, survival, and upgrade choices
Must have desktop display scaling set to 100% otherwise game will break
Still being balanced and polished

title: Tile Patterns
description: Cycle through animated tile patterns with recolourable sprite sets.
longDescription: This project creates a full-screen grid of rotating tile sprites. You can cycle through different procedural patterns, switch between sprite sets, and recolour the light and dark parts of the sprites using colour pickers.
controls: Click the left side to go to the previous pattern
Click the right side to go to the next pattern
Click the top centre reset area to return to normal
Open the menu to switch sprite sets and recolour the tiles
notes: Uses p5.js instead of the custom canvas boot system
Requires the band, fill, and merge sprite images to be in the project folder
Recoloured sprites are cached for better performance
